title: What is "Joining"? heart2heart (read person 2 person) focused coordinated movement, 2gather unison movement
body:
# What is "Joining"? heart2heart (read person 2 person) focused coordinated movement, 2gather unison movement

## OVERVIEW
@TonyLinog : Wanted to start this @joshualinog maybe you already have an issue for this?? And/or can modify/tag this issue to get it where you think it should go. 

## FEATURES OF JOINING
- steps:
     1. one person declares puts forward a proposed movement
      2. The other person accepts The proposal and decides to unite their movement to the proposal and movement of the initial person 
     3. Along the way the second person who decided to join, also casts vision over the proposed movement 
     4. The initial person embraces this and themselves continue casting vision over the movement as both persons attempt to move in unison together toward the proposal 
     5. Mutual submission takes place and .... (Don't remember your exact words @TonyLinog )

comments.author: TonyLinog
comments.body:
2 Corinthians 3:13-18;

 I read this briefly and sense it has to help us understand joining the 2-D with the 3-D somehow.

comments.author: joshualinog
comments.body:
> 2 Corinthians 3:13-18;
> 
>  I read this briefly and sense it has to help us understand joining the 2-D with the 3-D somehow. 


### passage: 2 Corinthians 3:13-18 LEB 
13 and not as Moses used to place a veil over his face, in order that the sons of Israel would not stare at the end of what was transitory. 14 But their minds were hardened. For until this very day, the same veil remains upon the reading of the old covenant, not being uncovered, because it is done away with in Christ. 15 But until today, whenever Moses is read aloud, a veil lies upon their heart, 16 but whenever one turns to the Lord, the veil is removed. 17 Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom. 18 And we all, with unveiled face, reflecting the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into the same image from glory into glory, just as from the Lord, the Spirit.
